title: Safety Auditing & Red-Teaming
concept: Safety auditing and red-teaming systematically probe vision systems with physical and digital attacks to measure safety boundaries.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main trade-off of adversarial training?

options.text: It improves robustness but slightly reduces clean image accuracy
It makes models smaller but increases latency
It works for classification but fails for segmentation

exercises.explain: Defending models against noise constraints often leads to a minor reduction in clean performance.

cards.title: Red-teaming your vision AI
cards.body: Before a bank opens, they hire ethical hackers to try to break into the vault. This is red-teaming. For vision AI, we must do the same: proactively attack our own models before bad actors do, discovering weaknesses in a safe testing environment!

cards.title: Safety Auditing & Red-Teaming
cards.body: Red-teaming is the practice of systematically attacking a production system to find flaws. In computer vision, this involves testing models against out-of-distribution data, physical patches, and digital gradient-based attacks (PGD) to evaluate safety.

cards.title: Crash testing a car
cards.body: You don't wait for a customer to crash to see if the airbags work. You put a dummy in the car and crash it into a concrete wall in a laboratory. Red-teaming is crash-testing your AI to measure its safety limits.

cards.title: Auditing a face-ID lock
cards.body: An audit team tests a smart lock. They try printed photos, digital screen displays, physical 3D masks, and adversarial patches. They discover the system ignores people wearing specific high-contrast sunglasses, exposing a critical vulnerability.

cards.title: Testing on validation set is not enough
cards.body: You might think a model is safe because it has 99% accuracy on your validation set. In reality, validation sets rarely contain adversarial noise, extreme lighting, or physical-world attacks, hiding critical safety flaws.

cards.title: Safety Audit Pipeline
cards.body: How teams audit vision models for safety:

left.text:
Adversarially trained weights
Input pre-filtering active
Multi-camera alignment
Measures safety margins

right.text:
PGD/FGSM digital noise
Printed physical patches
Out-of-distribution angles
Probes model weaknesses

exercises.prompt: What is the primary objective of red-teaming a vision system?

options.text: To proactively discover model vulnerabilities using deliberate attacks
To speed up the inference time on mobile hardware
To increase the training batch size
To export the model graph to ONNX format

exercises.explain: Red-teaming simulates real-world attack conditions to find weaknesses before deployment.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A model with high validation accuracy is guaranteed to be secure against physical adversarial patches.
exercises.explain: False. Normal validation sets do not test for adversarial attacks, so a model can have high accuracy but still fail under attack.

exercises.sentence: Systematically attacking your own AI system to test its security is called red-___.
exercises.explain: Red-teaming is an industry term for simulating adversarial attacks to verify safety.

exercises.prompt: Which test case should be included in a safety audit for a self-driving car's stop-sign detector?

options.text: Testing physical stop signs with varying graffiti and printed stickers
Testing the model on a different GPU architecture
Running the model with double the learning rate

exercises.explain: Testing with physical adjustments (stickers, graffiti) checks the model's robustness against real-world adversarial patches.

exercises.prompt: You are preparing to launch a face-verification gate for a bank. What is the most responsible steps to complete before release?

options.text: Perform a safety audit by red-teaming the system with physical patch masks and digital PGD attacks.
Launch the model immediately since the validation set score was 98%.
Quantize the model to INT8 and skip security testing to meet the deadline.
Train the model on cartoon drawings of faces.

exercises.explain: Next up: Object Detection Foundations. Conducting safety audits and red-teaming under realistic threat scenarios is essential to verify security before deploying face-verification systems in high-risk areas.
